title: PostgreSQL 18 Schema, Constraints, Keys, and Query-Driven Indexes · Compiler Case: Feed Ts Relational Domain
summary: The schema stores nested parser objects as JSON, URLs are mutable keys, tenant relationships are unchecked, text fields are unlimited, and indexes have no query evidence. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Predict Feed Ts Relational Domain · Theory Feed Ts Relational Domain
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 4595 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile relational entities, stable keys, constraints, tenant scope, and query-driven indexes; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Relational Domain model · Theory Feed Ts Relatio…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Relational Domain precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Relational Domain model · Theory Feed Ts Relatio…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5393 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile relational entities, stable keys, constraints, tenant scope, and query-driven indexes; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Relational Domain model · Theory Feed Ts Relatio…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One JSONB feed document preserves the best relational design.
